title: woof EPIC.md front-matter
description: YAML front-matter of epics/E<N>/EPIC.md within the project state root in the operator home, ~/.woof/state/projects/<project-key>/ (ADR-017). Captures Definition-stage commitments: observable outcomes (user-facing acceptance) and contract decisions (technical contracts realising those outcomes). Free-form prose may follow the front-matter; only the structured header is validated. After Definition closes, outcome IDs and contract-decision IDs are append-only; ID removal requires gate-driven deprecation. Contract decisions reference standard contract artefacts (OpenAPI documents, Pydantic models, JSON Schema files); Stage 5 Check 4 verifies that those references resolve under their native tooling before commit.

epic_id.description: Tracker-assigned epic identifier. Same value as plan.json.epic_id.

title.description: Short epic title; mirrors the tracker epic title.

intent.description: Optional tracker epic intent paragraph. When present, the Definition renderer uses this as the initial free-form prose on first push instead of deriving intent from the EPIC.md body.

acceptance_criteria.description: Prose statements that, taken together, define epic completion.

open_questions.description: Discovery-stage questions deliberately carried forward unresolved. Each item references an active OQ<n> from discovery/synthesis/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md and records why it remains deferred.

resolved_open_questions.description: Discovery-stage questions resolved during Definition. Together with open_questions[], this must cover every active OQ<n> in discovery/synthesis/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md when that file exists.

deprecated.description: True iff this outcome was deprecated post-Definition via gate revision. Deprecated IDs remain in the array for traceability; new work targets a replacement ID.

openapi_ref.description: JSON-Pointer-style reference into a project OpenAPI document. Example: 'spec/openapi.yaml#/paths/~1api~1v1~1comments/post'. Stage 5 Check 4 parses the document, resolves the pointer, and requires #/paths refs to point at operation-shaped objects with responses.

pydantic_ref.description: Module-and-class reference to a Pydantic model. Example: 'webapp/schemas/comment.py:CommentCreate'. Stage 5 Check 4 imports the target and verifies it is a pydantic.BaseModel subclass. For file-based refs whose project-only imports are unavailable in Woof's tool environment, Check 4 falls back to static source inspection for BaseModel/BaseSettings inheritance.

json_schema_ref.description: Path to a JSON Schema file. Example: 'schemas/audit-event.schema.json'. Stage 5 Check 4 compiles the schema with ajv-cli and validates top-level examples[] when present.

notes.description: Free-form prose. Use for rationale, not for contract content (which lives in the referenced artefact).
